sentiment: Greed in equities (CNN F&G 62.66), Extreme Fear in crypto (alternative.me F&G 28). S&P 500 at 7,403 above all key MAs (20d 7,274 / 50d 6,930 / 200d 6,787). VIX at 17.81, just below the 18 trending-bullish threshold. Market recovered strongly from March-April Iran/Hormuz shock via a rare V-bottom. Oil remains elevated at ~$95-100 WTI; gold near $4,570. Energy and consumer defensives leading; materials and utilities lagging.
fed_policy: Target rate 3.50-3.75% (unchanged through March and April 2026 meetings). Fed projects 1 cut in 2026, 1 in 2027 — data-dependent under new Chair Kevin Warsh. Next FOMC: June 16-17, 2026. ECB deposit rate 2.00% (March 19 meeting).
next_major_catalyst: FOMC Minutes release May 20 2026, US Housing Starts & Building Permits May 21, S&P Global Flash PMIs May 21, NVDA Q1 FY27 earnings May 20 AMC (rev ~$78-80B), WMT Q1 FY27 earnings May 21 BMO, June FOMC meeting Jun 16-17 2026.

strategy_adjustments.macro-volatility: Regime trending bullish: VIX 17.81, S&P above all MAs. Focus on this week's high-impact events — FOMC Minutes (May 20), Flash PMIs (May 21). Oil elevated at $95-100 supports energy-linked plays. Ride momentum direction on surprises; no defensive hedging needed at current VIX.
strategy_adjustments.earnings-momentum: Prime week for earnings momentum: NVDA reports May 20 AMC (78% YoY growth expected, ~90% beat probability on Polymarket). WMT May 21 BMO, TJX May 20. Bullish regime supports widening targets on strong beats. Favour large-cap tech and consumer bellwethers; require 3+ beat history.
strategy_adjustments.post-shock: Basic Materials sector down -5.84% this week (XLB below 50d MA, MACD negative). APD, NUE, FCX showing oversold conditions. Favour names with non-structural shocks, confirmed base above crash lows, and RSI below 40. Bullish regime means higher bar — genuine fundamental support required.
strategy_adjustments.crypto: BTC ~$77,000 (down ~5.8% past week from $81k high), ETH ~$2,128, SOL ~$85. Crypto Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) while equities show Greed — divergence creates selective opportunity. ETF inflows +$1.85B over 30 days though past 7 days saw $-1.27B outflows. Treat BTC as primary vehicle; maintain LOW-MODERATE conviction. Avoid overlapping with NVDA earnings volatility window (May 20).
strategy_adjustments.pair-trades: Trending bullish regime: favour sector rotation pairs — long tech/AI leaders vs short laggards. Energy vs consumer cyclical spread attractive given oil at $95-100 and CVX/XOM YTD outperformance. ISM Manufacturing 52.7 in April (expansionary 4th consecutive month) supports industrials long vs materials short.

notes: Regime classification per Trading Copilot thresholds: VIX 17.81 < 18 and S&P 500 above both 20d and 50d MA → trending bullish. S&P close 7,403.05 (May 18 2026, FT Markets).
